title: Cluster Design
description: The architectural anchor for a cluster. JSON is the source of truth; DESIGN.md is rendered. Decisions live in the project decision ledger and are cited by ADR ID, never restated. The structure array is machine-checkable: every brief file path is verified against it.

cluster.description: Cluster name, matching the directory under docs/design/

intention.description: The spirit of the work — what the world looks like when this is done. The anchor for every judgment call the documents don't explicitly cover. One to three paragraphs.

problem.description: What is broken or missing, who it affects, why now. Vague problems produce vague solutions — be specific about the pain.

solution.description: The design itself: how the pieces fit, integration points, data flow. Structure and rationale, not syntax — no code snippets or type signatures.

principles.description: Governing principles for this cluster (5-10 max) that resolve review questions before they are asked. Empty array when the project-level ADRs suffice.

decisions.description: ADR IDs this cluster introduced or is bound by. The ledger holds the content.

goals.description: What success looks like, 3-7 entries, each verifiable — you can tell whether it was achieved

text.description: The reasonable thing we are deliberately not doing

reason.description: Why it is excluded; empty string when obvious

non_goals.description: Scope protection — things a reader might expect to be in scope

brief.description: Brief ID that introduces this path; empty string for pre-existing paths

structure.description: The file layout, flat and machine-checkable. Every file path in every brief is verified against this list; two briefs claiming to create the same path is a check failure.

note.description: What exists here today and its state

inventory.description: What exists before this cluster touches it. Essential for porting/extraction work — kills false 'create' claims. Empty array for greenfield.

text.description: Specific and checkable — 'sub-millisecond normalization for paths under 1000 chars', not 'good performance'

constraints.description: Things that must not change: security requirements, performance expectations, cross-cutting conventions
